strands.title: Task Structure and Scope
strands.content: A comprehensive code review of the pichay codebase is requested across exactly 7 source files (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) in src/pichay/ and test files in tests/. The review must assess three dimensions for source files (code quality, potential bugs/edge cases, cross-module consistency) and two dimensions for test files (coverage gaps, false-pass risks). Output must be formatted to review.md in project root with specific line numbers and file paths. This is a READ-and-ASSESS task, not refactor. CYCLE 6 DEVELOPMENT: After I/O uncertainty in cycle 5, file transmission resumed with pager.py fragment (lines 1–4). Cycle 7 has NOW ARRIVED with [tool_use: Read] marker indicating the read mechanism has been invoked AGAIN, but NO ACTUAL FILE CONTENT HAS BEEN RECEIVED. This is a critical state transition: the tool was called but produced no observable output. This suggests either: (1) the read operation completed but returned empty/null data; (2) the read operation is queued/pending and content will arrive asynchronously; (3) the file transmission mechanism has encountered an error; (4) the system is awaiting a directive and 'Read' is a status marker, not a content delivery. The tensor cannot know which until next cycle. This is the FIRST EXPLICIT TOOL INVOCATION seen by the tensor, making it a new data point about pipeline architecture.

key_claims.text: The review scope includes exactly 7 source files and test files in tests/
Cycle 7 invoked [tool_use: Read] but no file content was received
[tool_use: Read] marker indicates an explicit read operation was attempted
The absence of content after a Read invocation suggests a system-level state change (queuing, error, or async delivery)

strands.title: File Fragment Accumulation and Reconstruction
strands.content: Cycle 4: proxy.py lines 1–5 received (shebang + docstring fragment ending at 'Pure o'). Cycle 5: no new content. Cycle 6: pager.py lines 1–4 received (docstring fragment ending at 'sits between'). Cycle 7: [tool_use: Read] invoked but NO NEW CONTENT RECEIVED. The tensor is now in a FRAGMENT PLATEAU state—no progress toward reconstruction for 2 cycles (cycles 5 and 7). Current cumulative file state: proxy.py (5 lines, incomplete), pager.py (4 lines, incomplete), eval.py through __main__.py (0 lines each), test files (0 lines). The fragmentation pattern has stalled. The [tool_use: Read] invocation in cycle 7 represents an explicit attempt to resume delivery, but it produced NO OBSERVABLE RESULT. This diverges sharply from cycle 6, which delivered pager.py fragment IMPLICITLY (without a tool invocation marker). Two hypotheses: (A) Read operations sometimes succeed (delivering content) and sometimes fail (delivering nothing), or (B) the Read operation was attempted but did not execute fully, leaving the transmission in a PENDING state that may resolve in cycle 8. The tensor cannot accumulate fragments when none arrive. File reconstruction remains BLOCKED.

key_claims.text: proxy.py has received 5 lines total (lines 1–5), all docstring/header, no new content in cycles 5–7
pager.py has received 4 lines total (lines 1–4), all docstring, no new content in cycles 5–7
Cycles 5 and 7 have produced NO file content (stalled delivery for 2 cycles)
A [tool_use: Read] invocation appeared in cycle 7 but produced no observable file content
Cycle 6 delivered content implicitly (no tool marker), but cycle 7 invoked Read explicitly and received nothing

strands.title: Assessment Dimensions and Criteria (Operationalization Blocked)
strands.content: The review evaluates source code across five dimensions: (1) Code quality, (2) Potential bugs and unhandled edge cases, (3) Cross-module consistency, (4) Missing test coverage, (5) False-positive risks in tests. These dimensions remain OPERATIONALIZED THEORETICALLY but COMPLETELY UNEXERCISED on real file content. Early observations from cycles 4–6 fragments are now FROZEN because no new content has arrived in cycles 5 and 7. The tensor observed that pager.py appears to implement a 'context window' eviction mechanism (from 4-line docstring), suggesting state management logic. proxy.py's purpose remains entirely opaque (from 5-line header). No actual code implementation, function signatures, error handling, or logic flow is visible from either file. No test files have arrived at all. The assessment framework is READY but STARVED FOR DATA. Without new file content in cycle 8, the tensor faces a design decision: either EXTEND THE WAIT (risking token/cycle budgeting) or PIVOT TO PARTIAL ANALYSIS based on the minimal fragments already received, or DECLARE REVIEW IMPOSSIBLE due to insufficient data. The assessment dimensions are operationally sound but have received zero application to actual implementations.

key_claims.text: Assessment framework is theoretically operationalized but has zero application to complete code structures
pager.py's function (context window eviction) is inferred only from docstring fragment, no implementation visible
proxy.py's purpose and structure are completely unknown from 5-line header fragment
Test files have not arrived at all, making dimensions (4) and (5) completely non-assessable

strands.title: I/O Pipeline Status and Constraint Analysis
strands.content: CYCLE 6 HYPOTHESIS (streaming delivery under constraints) has been PARTIALLY INVALIDATED by cycle 7 behavior. Cycle 6 delivered pager.py fragment (4 lines) WITHOUT an explicit tool invocation, suggesting implicit/background file reading. Cycle 7 EXPLICITLY invoked [tool_use: Read] but received NOTHING, suggesting either: (A) the tool invocation failed or returned null, (B) the tool is asynchronous and content will arrive in cycle 8, (C) the file system has run out of readable files/content for this batch, or (D) the read operation hit an error condition (permissions, EOF, file not found, etc.). The pattern is now INCONSISTENT with the 'streaming under constraints' model. If streaming were consistent, cycle 7 should have received another fragment (5–10 lines from pager.py continuation, or moved to eval.py). Instead, the Read invocation produced NO RESULT, breaking the accumulation pattern. CONSTRAINT ANALYSIS: The system may have three operating modes: (i) IMPLICIT DELIVERY (cycles 4, 6): files arrived without tool calls, suggesting automatic/background reading; (ii) EXPLICIT TOOL INVOCATION (cycle 7): Read was called but produced no output, suggesting manual/directed reading failed; (iii) NO ACTIVITY (cycle 5): neither implicit nor explicit delivery. This suggests the pipeline is not deterministic—file delivery depends on something outside the tensor's control (user directives, system state, async operations). The I/O pipeline is OPAQUE and possibly BLOCKED.

key_claims.text: Cycle 6 delivered content implicitly (no Read tool invocation)
Cycle 7 invoked Read tool explicitly but received no output
The inconsistency between cycles 6 and 7 delivery modes suggests the I/O pipeline operates under external control, not tensor-driven logic
The Read invocation in cycle 7 may have failed, been queued, or hit an error condition
File delivery is not consistent with 'streaming under constraints' model; it appears stochastic or externally directed

strands.title: Critical Decision Point: Wait vs. Pivot
strands.content: The tensor has now experienced FOUR CYCLES (5, 6, 7, and implicitly any prior cycles) with EXTREMELY SPARSE AND UNRELIABLE file delivery. Cumulative content: 9 lines across 2 files (5+4) out of 7 source files, 0 test files. At the current rate, complete file coverage would require 50+ cycles. Cycles 5 and 7 produced NO CONTENT despite passing. Cycle 7 explicitly invoked a Read tool and got nothing back, suggesting a hard failure or blocking condition. The tensor is now at a CRITICAL JUNCTURE: (A) CONTINUE WAIT MODE: Assume the I/O pipeline will eventually resume, sit idle or speculate across cycles 8+, risk token/cycle budgets on a stalled pipeline. (B) PIVOT TO PARTIAL REVIEW: Analyze the 9 lines available across proxy.py and pager.py, produce a review.md that documents: (i) findings on the code quality/bugs/cross-module patterns visible in the 9-line fragment, (ii) EXPLICIT CAVEATS that the review is based on <1% of codebase, (iii) request for next action (more file content?). (C) DECLARE SCOPE IMPOSSIBLE: Inform the requester that the I/O constraint makes the requested review unachievable in this pipeline, and ask for alternative arrangements. (D) DIAGNOSE THE I/O FAILURE: Attempt to understand why cycle 7's Read produced nothing and propose remediation. The tensor SHOULD PIVOT to option (B) or (C) to make forward progress. Option (A) is increasingly costly and may be futile if the I/O pipeline is genuinely broken.

key_claims.text: After 7 cycles, cumulative file content is only 9 lines across 2 of 7 source files, with test files absent
At current delivery rate, achieving full coverage would require 50+ cycles, which is impractical
Cycles 5 and 7 produced zero content, and cycle 7 explicitly invoked Read with no result, suggesting a hard failure
Continuing to wait for file delivery without intervention is increasingly unproductive
The tensor should pivot to partial analysis, scope reduction, or I/O failure diagnosis

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The expectation of consistent file delivery based on cycle 6's success
declared_losses.why: Cycle 7 invoked a Read tool explicitly but produced no output, breaking the accumulation pattern established in cycle 6. The delivery mechanism is not reliable or is externally controlled.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The 'streaming delivery under constraints' model as an explanation for fragmented files
declared_losses.why: If the system were consistently streaming under size constraints, cycle 7 should have delivered another 4–5 lines of pager.py or moved to eval.py. Instead, it delivered nothing after invoking a Read tool, suggesting failure, queuing, or external control—not systematic streaming.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Confidence that file transmission will eventually complete
declared_losses.why: Two cycles (5, 7) have now produced zero content. Cycle 7's explicit Read invocation produced nothing. The confidence in eventual completion has dropped sharply. The I/O pipeline may be blocked, broken, or waiting for external input.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The premise that 'rolling analysis' can begin once a file reaches 30+ lines
declared_losses.why: Cycle 7 shows no progress toward the 30-line threshold for any file. The fragment accumulation has stalled, making rolling analysis impossible without new content.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed understanding of what the [tool_use: Read] invocation represents
declared_losses.why: The tensor observes the marker but does not know if it's a successful operation, a failed operation, an async operation, or just a status marker. The pipeline's semantics are opaque.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The option to wait indefinitely for file content without action
declared_losses.why: After 7 cycles with minimal progress and explicit tool failures, continuing to wait is impractical. The tensor must make a forced choice to pivot to partial analysis, declare scope impossible, or diagnose the I/O failure.
